It  might just be my imagination,   but I do sense the tiniest of flexes in  the aluminum frame. Same when bent from   the back. But it's immediately obvious that  this less expensive, cheaper iPhone 17 passes   my durability test with flying colors. The  iPhone 17 is a ridiculously solid phone,   and if Apple keeps up this level of durability,  I'm going to be out of a job. At least now we   know what leaves permanent marks on the back  glass, though. Hit that subscribe button so   you don't miss the teardown, and thanks  a ton for watching. I'll see you around.
